business.headline: From Oman to Gujarat: The mega pipeline that could reshape India's energy futureEvery flare-up in the Strait of Hormuz sends shockwaves through global energy markets. Could a proposed 2,000-km undersea pipeline from Oman to Gujarat help India break free from that vulnerability? Let's find out.

Why the government cut Ujjwala LPG subsidy from nine to four cylinders a yearThe government has said the cut in subsidised Ujjwala LPG refills was driven by alleged diversion of cylinders for commercial use.

OpenAI files confidential IPO, eyes trillion-dollar valuation in AI boomThe company announced on Monday that it had submitted confidential paperwork for a stock market listing. However, OpenAI did not reveal the size of the offering, its expected valuation, or when it plans to go public.

Ujjawala LPG subsidy down to first 4 refills amid conflict-driven crude price surgeEarlier, PMUY beneficiaries received a Rs 300 subsidy on the first nine LPG refills annually. The government reduced this to four refills, citing average household consumption of about four cylinders per year.

India posts Q4 current account surplus as services exports surgeAccording to data released by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) on Monday, the country's current account recorded a surplus of USD 7.1 billion, or 0.7 per cent of GDP, in the fourth quarter of 2025-26.

Oil, growth, inflation: CEA explains India's key risks amid West Asia crisisSpeaking to India Today, CEA V Anantha Nageswaran said India's economy remains strong, but uncertainty around the ongoing conflict and energy prices continues to pose risks.

Air India may introduce 'no meals' tickets on domestic flights to cut faresAir India is considering a no-meals ticket category for domestic passengers. The plan could begin on select short routes and lower fares for flyers who skip onboard food.

Builder delayed possession? Here's what homebuyers can doBuilder delayed possession of your flat? Learn your rights under RERA, when you can seek compensation or a refund, whether you must continue paying EMI, and what legal and financial options homebuyers have when projects are delayed.

Oil prices cannot stay this high for long, says Oil Minister Hardeep Singh PuriWhile fuel prices remain elevated for now, Puri's comments suggest that policymakers expect the pressure to ease over time.

Want to invest in SpaceX's IPO? Here's what retail investors need to knowAccess to the SpaceX IPO is not limited to the United States. The company has indicated that qualified investors in several countries may be able to buy shares, subject to local regulations and eligibility requirements.

Sensex, Nifty hit 2-month lows as Iran-Israel tensions push crude oil near $100Markets closed at nearly two-month lows after fresh Israeli strikes on Iran and Lebanon pushed crude prices sharply higher. The sell-off deepened across sectors as investors weighed inflation risks, global tech weakness and the path of interest rates.

Economist's model got last 3 FIFA World Cup winners right. Here's its 2026 pickAs football fans debate whether Argentina can defend its title, whether England's golden generation can finally deliver, or whether France can add another star to its jersey, a German economist has come up with a prediction that is turning heads across the football world.

From AI trip planning to travel insurance: How are Indians redefining travel in 2026?Gone are the days when a holiday meant simply booking tickets and packing bags. Today's Indian traveller is more digital, more informed and more prepared, reshaping the travel landscape in the process.

India, US could finalise trade deal by July, says Commerce Minister Piyush GoyalThe Union Minister's remarks come at a time when India and the US are working to resolve outstanding issues in a proposed bilateral trade pact that is expected to improve market access and give Indian exporters an advantage over competitors in certain sectors.

AI stock sell-off hits Taiwan, South Korea after rally past IndiaAsian markets sank as investors dumped AI-linked chip stocks in Taiwan, South Korea and Japan. The sell-off has raised fresh questions over whether AI rally expectations had run ahead of earnings reality.

CIBIL score under 730? Getting a home or car loan could become tougher from 2027For many Indians, a home loan or car loan is often the biggest financial commitment they make. But from 2027, getting such loans could become more difficult if your CIBIL score is below 730, as banks prepare to follow stricter RBI guidelines on lending risk.

Adani Ports bags 10-year marine services deal for Argentina's first LNG exportsThe contract was awarded to Adani Harbour International FZCO, a step-down subsidiary of APSEZ, through a consortium formed with Argentina-based Meridian Group. The consortium won the contract after a global competitive bidding process conducted by Southern Energy S.A. (SESA).

Gold falls by Rs 1,800, silver crashes Rs 6,400: Why are precious metals falling?Gold and silver extended losses on Monday as stronger US jobs data altered rate expectations. The slide deepened as higher Treasury yields, Middle East tensions and inflation worries weighed on bullion.

Sensex falls 600 points: 3 reasons why stock market is falling todayMarkets opened sharply lower on Monday as rising Middle East tensions, higher crude prices and global weakness hit sentiment. The slide sharpened concerns over inflation, foreign outflows and pressure on technology stocks.

Stock market falls amid fresh Iran-Israel flare up, crude oil climbs above $96The selloff was broad-based, with almost all major stocks trading in the red. Investors turned cautious after fresh conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices higher and raised concerns about inflation and global economic growth.

entertainment.headline: Allu Arjun calls Ram Charan's Peddi act mind-blowing, sends love to Janhvi amid row Allu Arjun has praised Ram Charan's performance in Peddi, calling it a "mind-blowing" act and expressing pride in the actor's achievement. He also sent love to Janhvi Kapoor and lauded director Buchi Babu Sana for elevating the entire film.

Guru Randhawa joins LE SSERAFIM for first K-pop collaboration on BOOMPALA remix LE SSERAFIM have teamed up with Guru Randhawa for the first BOOMPALA remix. The release blends K-pop, Latin house and Punjabi-pop as the song gains global traction.

Sunny Deol renames Lahore 1947 as Batwara 1947, announces release date Sunny Deol has shared a major update on his upcoming collaboration with Aamir Khan and Rajkumar Santoshi. The film now has a new title and a release date that could hold special significance.

Amid Peddi row, Janhvi's Dharma film with Tiger Shroff, Lakshya gets release date Dharma Productions has announced the release date Lag Ja Gale, starring Janhvi Kapoor, Tiger Shroff and Lakshya. The announcement lands amid attention on Janhvi over the Peddi controversy.

Anushka Sharma, Virat Kohli make fan's day with handwritten note Anushka Sharma and Virat Kohli have won hearts once again with a thoughtful gesture for a fan in Ahmedabad. The couple not only posed for pictures with her but also left her a handwritten note, making the interaction even more special.

Why Made in India: A Titan Story is a must-watch for anyone who deeply fears failure The freedom of failure is the courage to try again, fail again, and fail better. That honest space turns hesitation into momentum and quiet dreams into enduring legacies. Made in India: A Titan Story addresses the fears of every dreamer scared of the first wrong tick.

Tanmay Bhat's apparent Ranveer Singh jab leaves Varun Dhawan in splits A joke, a burst of laughter and one viral reaction. Tanmay Bhat's latest comment involving Varun Dhawan has reignited conversation around the ongoing Don 3 controversy and left social media reading between the lines.

Sandhya Mridul calls out discrimination after house help denied salon service Sandhya Mridul said her mother had to plead with two salons before a young domestic worker was given a haircut. She said the incident exposed a wider discriminatory mindset towards domestic staff in everyday spaces.

RGV's Obsession theory says every woman has Nikki within her, internet doesn't buy Ram Gopal Varma's posts praising Obsession and explaining Nikki's appeal drew sharp criticism on X. The response turned his interpretation of the film's success into a broader debate about the portrayal of women.

Peddi row: Janhvi's sexualised scenes removed, Buchi Babu says people misread them Buchi Babu Sana revealed in an interview that he has finally removed problematic scenes from Peddi after criticism over Janhvi Kapoor's portrayal. The move followed objections to shots and a romance scenes seen as disrespectful towards women.

The Dog Stars trailer shows Jacob Elordi's quest for hope in post-apocalyptic world Jacob Elordi takes centre stage in the first trailer for Ridley Scott's The Dog Stars. The new footage teases a mysterious journey through a dangerous post-apocalyptic world ahead of the film's release.

Actor Manjari Fadnis breaks down, alleges society dog was hit on head with rod Actor Manjari Fadnis shared an emotional video alleging that some people in her society brutally attacked a community dog named Mikey. Her account has drawn attention to Mikey's disappearance, an alleged confession and called for stronger animal cruelty laws.

Peddi box office Day 5: Ram Charan's film to enter Rs 300 crore-club worldwide Ram Charan's Peddi stayed in double digits on its first Monday and reached Rs 169.70 crore India nett. The film has crossed several lifetime totals and is now moving towards the India nett mark of They Call Him OG.

Kangana Ranaut admits to self-doubt in politics: Am I still too much of a heroine? BJP MP and actor Kangana Ranaut opened up about the self-doubt she experiences as a politician, revealing that she often questions whether she is meeting expectations in her new role.

Bandar irony: Bobby Deol's career-best role comes in a film no one's looking at In Bandar, Bobby Deol sheds familiar glamour to get into the skin of a role destined to highlight his career. The problem: Anurag Kashyap's grim film is yet to get people talking.

Katy Perry, Justin Trudeau break the internet with PDA-filled red carpet debut Katy Perry and Justin Trudeau have taken their romance to the next level with their first-ever red carpet appearance together at the Tribeca Festival. Here's what happened and why fans can't stop talking about it.

The Bear Season 5 trailer brings floods, financial woes and one last shot at success The Bear season 5 trailer teases mounting pressure, a flooded restaurant and a race against time. As the final season approaches, the team faces its toughest challenge yet.

Peddi isn't alone; KGF, Pushpa and the blockbuster habit of objectifying women The backlash against Peddi has put the spotlight on the portrayal of Janhvi Kapoor's character. More importantly, it has revived a larger conversation about how India's biggest blockbusters continue to sideline women in favour of larger-than-life heroes.

Dhanush calls Rafael Nadal his GOAT after watching Rafa documentary Dhanush's reaction to the Netflix documentary Rafa has caught fans' attention. The actor's emotional message reveals a new perspective on Rafael Nadal's career and legacy.

general news.headline: Tamil Nadu Speaker rejects disqualification calls for 21 AIADMK Shamugam camp MLAs who backed TVKTamil Nadu Speaker rejects disqualification calls for 21 AIADMK Shamugam camp MLAs who backed TVK

Govt clears new Rs 13,000-crore Great Nicobar airport, drops INS Baaz expansionThe new airport will be built at Chingen near Galathea Bay and is expected to serve both civilian and military aviation requirements. Defence sources said the decision followed studies that found extending INS Baaz's existing 4,500-foot runway to around 10,000 feet was impractical because of topography and infra-related constraints.

Yusuf Pathan's silence screams loudest as Trinamool implodesYusuf Pathan finds himself at the centre of the Trinamool Congress's growing rebellion as rival factions battle for control of the party. Accused by some of siding with the rebels and criticised by loyalists for his silence, the Berhampore MP faces mounting pressure to clarify where his political loyalties lie.

Footballer detained, referee deported: FIFA World Cup is a mess before kick-offJust days before the beginning of football's greatest spectacle, the FIFA World Cup has run into a myriad of controversies. The latest of those involves Africa's best football referee.

Rs 2.5 lakh Japan school trip costs more than 25 years of Kendriya Vidyalaya feesA viral social media post about a Class 8 student's Rs 2.5 lakh school trip to Japan has revived a familiar debate: when educational experiences start costing more than education itself. The amount is so large that it could cover the annual fees of a Kendriya Vidyalaya student for more than two decades.

SL A vs IND A Live: Ruturaj scores 21st List A hundred and departsSri Lanka A vs India A live score: India A (228/5 in 45 ovs). Openers Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Prabhsimran Singh have lost their wickets inside the first 5 overs of the match. Sri Lanka have been sensational with their catching and ground fielding so far, which has made the difference in this tour game so far. However, Ruturaj and Tilak have now started to build a brilliant partnership.

iPhone almost like a birth control device, fertility rates falling after 2007: ResearchGlobal birth rates have been declining, and new studies believe that your iPhone may be the culprit. Research hints that the iPhone may have played a role in falling birth rates ever since it was first launched in 2007.

Poison for profit? Why toxic agrochemicals still flow freely across IndiaDozens of countries have banned hazardous pesticides. In India, many remain in use, fuelling a growing battle over public health, farm economics and corporate accountability.
Haridwar family finds 27 snake hatchlings in water tank, watch rescue videoResidents of a Haridwar home were left stunned after spotting 27 snake hatchlings inside their water tank.

Elon Musk flags India's declining fertility rate. What's driving the dip? How bad is it?Tesla boss Elon Musk's post on India's falling fertility rate has revived the debate over why fewer children are being born in India. Experts say the decline in the total fertility rate (TFR) is reflective of rising living costs, financial development, urban lifestyles and changing family aspirations and dynamics.

Protests in UK over POK killings, 30 British MPs express concern overKashmiri protesters demonstrated in front of the Pakistani consulate in Bradford, the UK, over the firing on mourners at a Rawalakot funeral in Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir (POK) in which at least 11 people were killed. This came even as 30 British MPs sought diplomatic intervention over the massive human rights abuse in POK.

Why the government cut Ujjwala LPG subsidy from nine to four cylinders a yearThe government has said the cut in subsidised Ujjwala LPG refills was driven by alleged diversion of cylinders for commercial use.

Renault Duster TCe100 review, first review: 1-litre turbo-petrol punches above its weightThe Renault Duster is available with a 1.3-litre turbo-petrol engine, while a strong hybrid will arrive later this year and the Duster is also available with a 1-litre turbo engine. But is the 1-litre turbo-petrol Duster worthy of your consideration? Let's find out.

Live: TMC MPs June Malia, Dev attend Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari's meetingWest Bengal TMC Crisis Live Updates: The Trinamool Congress' deepening internal crisis spilled into the open on Tuesday as the party leadership and a rebel faction traded sharp attacks amid claims that a group of MPs is preparing to back the BJP-led NDA in Parliament. At a press conference, TMC Lok Sabha Chief Whip Kalyan Banerjee dismissed the dissidents as "gaddars" and "traitors", daring them to resign from the party and seek a fresh mandate from voters. Banerjee claimed the rebels stood "exposed" the moment they met senior BJP leader Bhupender Yadav and accused them of abandoning party workers while enjoying the privileges of office. He also took aim at rebel MP Sharmila Sarkar, alleging she had remained silent on issues such as the RG Kar hospital controversy. The remarks came hours after rebel leader and Lok Sabha MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar claimed that nearly 20 Trinamool MPs had decided to write to Speaker Om Birla expressing support for the NDA. The dissident camp is expected to argue that Ghosh Dastidar remains the party's chief whip in the Lok Sabha, setting the stage for a high-stakes battle over the Trinamool's parliamentary leadership and future. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the live updates.

Swing from secular to Hindutva: How BJP won over the Bengali BhadralokThe BJP's 2026 breakthrough in West Bengal drew strong backing from the Bhadralok, long seen as sceptical of Hindutva politics. The shift reflected anti-incumbency against the Trinamool Congress, anger over jobs and corruption, and the impact of RG Kar protests and RSS mobilisation.

Times have changed, pre-marital sex no ground to judge character: SCIn a ruling that could influence everything from criminal cases to government jobs, the Supreme Court has challenged long-held assumptions about pre-marital relationships, consent and whether a failed romance can define a person's character.

Delhi's hottest new nightlife trend isn't a speakeasy. It's gallery hoppingDelhi's latest Friday-night crawl swaps cocktails for canvases, though, as it turns out, you might not have to give up the G&T after all.

TheLiverDoc says his Instagram posts criticising homeopathy are blockedA fresh controversy has erupted after hepatologist and social media educator TheLiverDoc claimed that one of his Instagram posts criticising homeopathy was blocked in India. The development has reignited debate over scientific criticism, free speech and the regulation of alternative medicine.

Trouble continues for DKS, ministers unhappy with portfolios rush to DelhiThe Ramalinga Reddy crisis may be over, but fresh signs of unease have emerged within the Karnataka Cabinet. With ministers heading to Delhi over portfolio concerns, new challenges are surfacing for Chief Minister DK Shivakumar.

Hum Saath Saath Nahin Hain: Honeymoon turns into family trip, bride seeks divorceA dream honeymoon turned into a nightmare when a newlywed bride discovered she would be sharing the trip with her husband's parents and siblings. The fallout has reached counselling sessions, with divorce now on the table.

1 lakh customers bought this SUV: Offers 28kmpl efficiency, price starts at Rs 10.50 lakhMaruti Suzuki's latest SUV has crossed the 1 lakh sales milestone in just eight months. Priced from Rs 10.50 lakh and offering up to 28.65kmpl efficiency, the SUV has quickly become a strong seller.

What made Usha Vance want baby no. 4? JD Vance revealsIn his forthcoming book, JD Vance says Usha Vance reconsidered having a fourth child after Charlie Kirk's death and a conversation with his widow, Erika Kirk. The account differs from Vance's earlier assertion that he was "persuasive" and from Usha's own statement that she had never closed the door on another baby.

Man buys apartment on 34th floor, spoiler: the building has only 32 floorsA man in China discovered his supposed 34th-floor flat was in a building officially listed with only 32 floors. The case has renewed scrutiny of limited-property-rights housing and property record checks.

Peddi row: Janhvi's sexualised scenes removed, Buchi Babu says people misread themBuchi Babu Sana revealed in an interview that he has finally removed problematic scenes from Peddi after criticism over Janhvi Kapoor's portrayal. The move followed objections to shots and a romance scenes seen as disrespectful towards women.

sports.headline: Footballer detained, referee deported: FIFA World Cup is a mess before kick-off Just days before the beginning of football's greatest spectacle, the FIFA World Cup has run into a myriad of controversies. The latest of those involves Africa's best football referee.

Bangladesh T20 captain reacts to WC boycott: Can play in Pak, then why not India? Litton Das has challenged Bangladesh's official line on pulling out of the T20 World Cup in India over security. His account raises fresh questions about why the call was eventually made.

SL A vs IND A Live: Ruturaj scores 21st List A hundred and departs Sri Lanka A vs India A live score: India A (217/4 in 43.4 ovs). Openers Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Prabhsimran Singh have lost their wickets inside the first 5 overs of the match. Sri Lanka have been sensational with their catching and ground fielding so far, which has made the difference in this tour game so far. However, Ruturaj and Tilak have now started to build a brilliant partnership.

No rivals, beyond medals: How yogasana became more than a hunt for glory At the first World Yogasana Championship, athletes and coaches from 78 countries competed while often helping one another on and off the mat. The event suggested yogasana's strongest claim is not just medals, but a transnational culture of family, self-improvement and shared purpose.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is back: When and where to watch India A vs Sri Lanka A Vaibhav Sooryavanshi returns as India A open the Dambulla tri-series against Sri Lanka A on Tuesday. Tilak Varma will lead a young Indian side in the tri-series, which will also feature Afghanistan A side.

What really happened at London nightclub Ben Stokes and England teammate visited Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson are being investigated after a late-night altercation in London following England's win over New Zealand. The episode has delayed England's second-Test selection and reopened uneasy questions over leadership, discipline and control.

No Virat Kohli. Will Rohit Sharma play ODIs vs Afghanistan? India are awaiting medical clearance on Rohit Sharma for the Afghanistan ODI series. Hardik Pandya has been cleared, while Virat Kohli has been ruled out with a hamstring tear.

You're a gift to the sport: Andre Agassi hails Chwalinska's Roland Garros run Andre Agassi delivered an emotional message to Maja Chwalinska after her dream run to the Roland Garros final came to an end. The American legend hailed the Polish qualifier's historic achievement and called her "a gift to the sport."

Ben Stokes in trouble? England cricket probe 'nightclub incident' after Lord's Test England captain Ben Stokes and pacer Gus Atkinson are under investigation after an alleged breach of team protocols following the first Test against New Zealand. The incident has delayed squad decisions for the second Test and revived scrutiny of England's off-field culture.

Mandaviya backs club-led model to secure ISL future Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya has asked the AIFF and ISL clubs to form a joint task force to settle their financial dispute. The move is aimed at securing a stable business model before the 2026-27 season begins.

Africa's best referee denied entry to USA, set to be ruled out of FIFA World CupAfrica's top referee Omar Abdulkadir Artan has reportedly been denied entry into the United States and sent back to Turkey ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The incident has intensified concerns over visa and travel issues affecting tournament personnel.

They said you'd never win: Djokovic writes moving tribute to champion ZverevFollowing Alexander Zverev's historic French Open breakthrough, Novak Djokovic wrote a deeply personal tribute to his long-time friend, lauding the German's resilience in silencing critics who claimed he would never win a Grand Slam.

Shreyaka takes 4 as India spin to win against West Indies in T20 World Cup warm-upWomen's T20 World Cup 2026: Bharti Fulmali's unbeaten fifty and Shreyanka Patil's four wickets gave India a 26-run warm-up win over West Indies in Cardiff. The outing offered useful game time to India's bench before matches against England and Pakistan.

Suryakumar Yadav answers T20I snub with Mumbai T20 League blitzDropped from India's T20I squads for Ireland and England, Suryakumar Yadav struck an unbeaten 72 in the Mumbai T20 League at Wankhede. The innings revived his run of form and renewed focus on his place in India's plans.

India's delicate plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Help his game, not ruin itIndia's batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will be eased into the national set-up after his maiden T20I call-up. The team wants to protect the 15-year-old's natural game rather than overload him with advice.

Test team in transition? Sunil Gavaskar disagrees with coach Gautam Gambhir's takeAs India enter a crucial World Test Championship phase, batting legend Sunil Gavaskar has urged the young, evolving batting unit to step up and focus on long-form discipline rather than relying on the transition narrative.

Christian Eriksen in good spirits, set for discharge after mid-match heart scareChristian Eriksen is expected to be discharged from hospital soon after making a positive recovery from a mid-match collapse during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine. The Danish midfielder quickly regained consciousness, with doctors confirming he remains in stable condition.

Yogasana Super League: An IPL-themed competition to boost yoga's Olympic dreamYogasana Bharat says the Yogasana Super League is nearly ready for launch this year. The league sits at the centre of a broader push for Asian Games, Commonwealth Games and Olympic recognition.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's rise is dangerous for cricket: Greg ChappellFormer Australia cricketer Greg Chappell has warned that T20 cricket is approaching a tipping point, arguing that the format has become heavily skewed in favour of batters. Concerned about the growing imbalance between bat and ball, Chappell proposed three rule changes that he believes could restore balance to the game.

Gill reveals India's 350-run, 20-wicket Test formula after AFG routShubman Gill laid out India's 350-run, 20-wicket Test formula after the innings win over Afghanistan in Mullanpur. The result underlined India's bench strength and highlighted Manav Suthar's dream debut in a side in transition.

Manav Suthar stays grounded despite seven-wicket India Test debutManav Suthar starred on his India Test debut as his seven wickets sealed a crushing win over Afghanistan. Yet the left-arm spinner said the format demands patience, consistency and humility.

Innings and 300 runs: Suthar blows away Afghans inside 3 days on Test debutShubman Gill's India registered their biggest victory in Test cricket on Monday, June 8. Debutant Manav Suthar blew away Afghanistan with 7 wickets in Mullanpur.

Ignored for India, Shami makes his presence felt with hattrick in Bengal T20 LeagueMohammed Shami took a Bengal T20 League hat-trick for Siliguri Strikers against Rarh Tigers. The spell revived scrutiny of India's selection calls as the veteran remains outside ODI and Test plans.

Angry Mohammed Siraj riles up Rahmat Shah, triggers Afghanistan collapseIND vs AFG: Mohammed Siraj triggered Afghanistan's collapse on Day 3 of the Test match. He did not do it with his bat or ball, but with his mere words.

India captain snaps back at reporter over retirement question at Women's T20 WCHarmanpreet Kaur brushed aside retirement talk with a joke at a pre-tournament captains' media session. The exchange kept the focus on India's T20 World Cup build-up and the wider lift success has given women's cricket at home.

CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa, impresses Norway Chess championTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay felicitated R Praggnanandhaa in Chennai, awarded him Rs 50 lakh and played an impromptu game of chess. The meeting followed the grandmaster's historic Norway Chess title win, with Praggnanandhaa saying he was surprised by the Chief Minister's skill.

Manav Suthar becomes 2nd Indian this century to pick 5 wickets on Test debutIndia's debutant left-arm spinner ripped through Afghanistan with 6/33 and helped enforce the follow-on. The spell, backed by a handy 28, put him among India's best Test debutants.

Let Sooryavanshi be, don't expect the world of him: Sourav GangulyFormer India captain Sourav Ganguly has asked the world to temper his expectations ahead of Vaibhav Sooryavanshi potential India debut. Ganguly has said that playing for India will be different from playing in the IPL.

Flavio Cobolli follows Andreeva's example, thanks himself after French Open finalFrench Open 2026: Flavio Cobolli thanked himself and praised Alexander Zverev after a five-set French Open final defeat. The loss ended his title bid but confirmed a breakthrough fortnight that changed his standing in the game.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
